# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment YAML for our Python web application. The app runs a Flask API on port 5000. We need 3 replicas for high availability. Keep the configuration simple for quick deployment to our production cluster.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
 name: python-flask-app   # Define a suitable unique resource identifier  (mandatory)   
 namespace: default       # The associated kubernetes Namespace in which this pod will be deployed. Also, the main way to manage resources across different namespaces is through 'namespace' field on each object definition eith customResourceDefinition v1 beta and clusterRoleBinding / ClusterRole . (optional when there are multiple namespace)
spec:
 replicas: 3               # Specify how many pods should be created.   ReplicaSets automatically create new Pods whenever the number of existingPod is less than desired count, up to maximum size specified by 'maxSurge' in a Deployment spec (optional with minReadySeconds) .
 selector:                // A Label query over pods that must select using `spec` field. These labels are combined with  and taken as events by the Controller. Fields like imageTag, matchLabels , etc., can be used to make sure it works for all Kubernetes versions(optional if there is only one Deployment spec)
   matchLabels:            // Label query over pods that will select our deployment's Pod template (mandatory). This could include additional keys and values as needed.  For example, we would tag the templates with image=foo & version=bar so they can be re-created in different configurations if necessary
     app: python          # Match all replicas associated to a pod named "python" .    The key must match exactly (optional)   Must Be matched by selector.replica as it is an alias for the metadata name  field on Deployments and ReplicationControllers.(mandatory when there are more than one namespace).
       version: v1         # Match all replicas associated to a pod named "python-v2" . Labels must match exactly, we cannot use selectors here. Must be matched by selector with key app & value python (optional if only having single ReplicaSet)     MUST BE the alias name for 'metadata' field on Deployments and their sub resources(mandatory).
   template:                # Defines what these Pods should look like  in-depth within a podTemplateSpec. This is essentially how new containers are created every time an deployment runs (optional if only having one ReplicaSet) . It will run with the same configuration as for our app when it gets started up, and has enough resources to start serving requests on port 5000
     metadata:            # Additional labels or annotations that might be configured in this template. For example ('app': 'python','version': v1)   (optional if there are only one ReplicaSet). Labels of Pod templates should not include resource-limits & nodeSelectors fields because these would lead to errors due them already being set by the Deployment controller(mandatory when multiple namespace and/or single replica exists as a part from our app's metadata)
       labels:             // The 'app', with an optional value (e.g., python-v1,python).   For all copies of this template that will be used in different pod configurations for example one version or the other if needed(optional when there are multiple ReplicaSets exist as part from our app's metadata)
         name: flaskApp     // 'app', with an optional value (e.g., python-v1).  For all copies of this template that will be used in different pod configurations for example one version or the other if needed(optional when there are multiple ReplicaSets exist as part from our app's metadata)
         image: <image>    // Required, replace with your docker images name.   (mandatory without multi-namespace configuration).  This could be a Docker Hub user repository url or another location that the Kubernetes can pull/promise Image(optional when there are only one ReplicaSet)
         ports:             # Specifies how many port numbers and protocols should expose each container. A list of all available networked devices is exposed (mandatory).  Ports must be unique within a Pod - this ensures that multiple containers in the same pod run on different ports can't communicate with one another due to 'port collision'.
           - protocol: TCP   // The port used by each container. Defaults to incoming connection(optional)     Can use either "TCP" or "UDP".  If you set it explicitly then your network configuration must include this field in its outgoing traffic too (mandatory).    For example, In a Deployment with multiple pod replicas all containers expose port '5001' if we select to not specify the `ports` at deployment level or only use one container and try connecting.  Kubernetes will throw an error due this missing field(optional)
             containerPort:   // The number on which each Pod listens for connections (mandatory). This could be between '1' - '65535', the exact values allowed by your particular implementation of service protocols, depending upon how you configure services in Kubernetes.  For example when using a TCP protocol containerPort should match with port number on which it runs to get request from external world (mandatory)
               targetPort:   // The 'container-port' that each Pod listens for connections and handles requests against ('/'). This could be between the same range as above, but must not exceed total available ports in your service(optional).  For example when using a TCP protocol if we select to listen on port number `501` then it needs an entry at 'targetPort' of type integer (mandatory)
                 name: flask-service # Required for each outgoing connection, the unique identifier within all Pods/containers. Must be one amongst your pod names and must match with containerName in incoming traffic(optional).  If no `containerNames` is defined then 'targetPort' should use as its alias (mandatory)
    imagePullPolicy: Always # Required, replace by the actual value if you want to pull images from a private Docker registry or other location. Defaults to always pulling Images(optional when there are only one ReplicaSet). For multiple namespaces it must be specified in 'image' section of each Pod template (mandatory)
    containers:          # Required, list all the different components within your application such as database connections etc., If this is not provided we will get an error due to missing required fields.   Can include many things depending upon how complex you are using it in a flask app e.g multiple data-containers and only one web container
      - name: python  # Required, the unique identifier for each specific component within your application such as db or iframes etc., Must match with 'name' defined at podTemplateSpec level(mandatory).   (optional when there are no multi namespace configurations)        It could include all these like image , ports and name in same way.
          image: <image> # Required, replace by the actual value  if you want to pull images from a private Docker registry or other location otherwise it will use your local machine's Image (mandatory without multi-namespace configuration).   This could be a user repository url for dockerhub in case of remote image. For example kubernetes/python:3.7
          ports:       # Specifies how many port numbers and protocols should expose each container within that pod, which listens on an IP address at what machine can accept connections from outside(mandatory). Ports must be unique for different containers in the same Pod (optional when there are only one ReplicaSet)   This could include all these like image , ports or name.
            - port: 5001 # Required, to expose that number on what IP machine can connect from outside(mandatory). Must match with 'containerPort' in the same pod definition at template level (optional when there are only one ReplicaSet)   This could be any integer between `port` and total available ports defined by kubernetes services.
              name: flask-service # Required for each incoming connection, unique within all Pods/containers named here(mandatory). Must match with 'targetPort' in the same pod definition at template level (optional when there are only one ReplicaSet)   For example K8S_SERVICE_PORT is used as a default value of such field.
